skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, Grok and 40+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Seedance 1.5 Pro, Wan 2.5, Grok Imagine Video, OmniHuman, Fabric, HunyuanVideo. Capabilities: text-to-video, image-to-video, lipsync, avatar animation, video upscaling, foley sound. Use for: social media videos, marketing content, explainer videos, product demos, AI avatars. Triggers: video generation, ai video, text to video, image to video, veo, animate image, video from image, ai animation, video generator, generate video, t2v, i2v, ai video maker, create video with ai, runway alternative, pika alternative, sora alternative, kling alternative
skill.summary: Generate professional AI videos using 40+ models including Google Veo, Seedance, Wan, and Grok through the inference.sh CLI platform.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 43 external command patterns and 19 URLs, but all are FALSE POSITIVES. The external commands are bash code blocks in documentation showing usage examples, not actual shell execution. URLs are documentation links to inference.sh resources. The skill uses allowed-tools: Bash(infsh *) which properly restricts bash commands to the inference.sh CLI only. No malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Documentation contains shell command examples
low_findings.description: The skill documentation includes bash code blocks showing CLI usage. These are documentation examples only, not executable code. The skill restricts bash access to 'infsh *' commands via allowed-tools directive.

low_findings.title: Documentation contains external URLs
low_findings.description: Multiple URLs link to inference.sh documentation and resources. These are reference links for users, not network calls made by the skill itself.

content.value_statement: Transform text prompts and images into professional videos using cutting-edge AI models like Google Veo, Seedance, and Wan. No video editing skills required - generate marketing content, social media videos, and AI avatars in minutes.
actual_capabilities: Generate videos from text prompts using Google Veo 3.1, Seedance, Wan, and Grok models
Animate static images into videos with image-to-video models
Create AI avatars with lipsync from photos and audio files
Upscale low-quality videos using Topaz AI upscaler
Add sound effects and foley audio to silent videos
Merge multiple video clips with transition effects
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI installation and account authentication
Video generation depends on external API availability and rate limits
Cannot process videos longer than model-specific duration limits
Output quality depends on input prompt clarity and image resolution

use_cases.title: Social Media Content Creator
use_cases.description: Generate engaging short-form videos for TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts using text prompts describing the desired scene.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: Marketing Video Production
use_cases.description: Create product demo videos and explainer content without hiring videographers or using stock footage.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams and small business owners

use_cases.title: AI Avatar and Talking Head Videos
use_cases.description: Generate professional presenter videos with synchronized lipsync from static portraits and voice audio.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a video using Google Veo 3.1 Fast with the prompt: A drone shot flying over a dense forest at sunrise, cinematic lighting, 4K quality
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner - Create your first AI video with a simple text description

prompt_templates.title: Image Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this image using Wan 2.5: [upload image URL]. Make the water flow naturally and add subtle cloud movement in the sky.
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate - Bring a static landscape photo to life

prompt_templates.title: AI Avatar with Narration
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a talking head video using OmniHuman 1.5 with this portrait [image URL] and this voice recording [audio URL]. Ensure natural facial expressions and accurate lip synchronization.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Step Video Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: First generate a 5-second video of ocean waves using Grok Video. Then add foley sound effects with the prompt: crashing waves, seagulls, wind. Finally upscale the result using Topaz Video Upscaler to 4K resolution.
prompt_templates.scenario: Expert - Chain multiple video operations for polished results

output_examples.input: Generate a timelapse video of a flower blooming in a garden
output_examples.output: Video file generated via Google Veo 3.1 Fast showing a smooth timelapse of a flower blooming from bud to full bloom in a sunlit garden setting, approximately 5 seconds duration at 24fps

output_examples.input: Animate this portrait photo with the attached voice recording
output_examples.output: Talking head video created with OmniHuman 1.5 featuring natural facial movements and synchronized lip movements matching the provided audio, output as MP4 file

best_practices: Write detailed prompts including camera angles, lighting conditions, and motion descriptions for better video quality
Use high-resolution source images (minimum 720p) when creating image-to-video content
Test with shorter duration settings first before generating full-length videos to save credits
anti_patterns: Do not use vague prompts like 'make a video' without specific visual details
Avoid using low-resolution or heavily compressed images as input for animation
Do not chain too many processing steps in a single request - break complex workflows into separate commands

faq.question: Do I need to install the inference.sh CLI to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill requires the inference.sh CLI to be installed and authenticated. Run 'curl -fsSL https://cli.inference.sh | sh' followed by 'infsh login' to set up.

faq.question: Which AI video models are available through this skill?
faq.answer: The skill supports 40+ models including Google Veo 3.1, Seedance 1.5 Pro, Wan 2.5, Grok Imagine Video, OmniHuman for avatars, and specialized tools for upscaling and lipsync.

faq.question: How long does video generation typically take?
faq.answer: Generation time varies by model and video length. Fast models like Veo 3.1 Fast can generate 5-second clips in under a minute, while higher quality models may take several minutes.

faq.question: Can I use my own images and audio files?
faq.answer: Yes, upload your files to a publicly accessible URL and reference them in the input. The skill supports image-to-video, avatar creation with custom portraits, and lipsync with your own audio.

faq.question: What video formats and resolutions are supported?
faq.answer: Output is typically MP4 format. Resolution depends on the model - Veo 3 supports up to 1080p, Seedance Pro supports 1080p output, and the Topaz upscaler can enhance to 4K.

faq.question: Is there a free tier or trial available?
faq.answer: Check the inference.sh platform for current pricing and trial options. Some models may offer free credits for new users.
